Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

This is the wrong way to solve this. libcfs_ip_str2addr() needs to fixed to find the correct address.

Patch Set 1: Verified+1

Passed enforced test review-ldiskfs-dne-arm on RHEL 8.8/aarch64, RHEL 8.8/x86_64 uploaded by Onyx Autotest 2 from onyx-91vm3: https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/b9ab5d11-81cc-4711-8b58-ac6e11d01bd5 ran 5 tests.

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1
> 
> This is the wrong way to solve this. libcfs_ip_str2addr() needs to fixed to find the correct address.

The issue isn't that libcfs_ip_str2addr() is wrong. It's that this function seemingly never gets called in the current code. In fact, libcfs_str2nid() used by this patch is just libcfs_ip_str2addr() under the hood.

If you look at how yaml_lnet_ping() is called, it takes the command line arguments directly and interprets them as an array of NIDs without any parsing. So if any of the arguments are host names, they don't get resolved. If the arguments are comma-separated, they don't get split. The non-netlink version correctly handles these cases.

Patch Set 1:

This is a general problem. Can you try this with peers? If it doesn't work we can try fixing lustre_lnet_parse_nid_range() instead. Once fixed we can update ping to use lustre_lnet_peer_nid_range(). The bonus with using lustre_lnet_parse_nid_range() is that we can use 10.0.1.[1-20] for example.

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> This is a general problem. Can you try this with peers? If it doesn't work we can try fixing lustre_lnet_parse_nid_range() instead. Once fixed we can update ping to use lustre_lnet_peer_nid_range(). The bonus with using lustre_lnet_parse_nid_range() is that we can use 10.0.1.[1-20] for example.

What do you want me to try? I'm not sure I understand. I tested between two separate nodes. Even testing `lnetctl ping` against a real filesystem doesn't work.

I can take a look at lustre_lnet_parse_nid_range().

Patch Set 1:

Setting up a peer using a hostname. Then seeing which IP address it picked.

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

This change cannot be cherry-picked to master.
Please rebase the change locally and upload the rebased commit for review.

commit.subject: LU-17629 lnet: netlink lnetctl ping doesn't resolve hostname
commit.message:
LU-17629 lnet: netlink lnetctl ping doesn't resolve hostname

In my test environment, each node is assigned two IPs via
DHCP - one IP per private network (192.168.121.0/24 and
172.28.128.0/24). The /etc/hosts file is set such that
the 172.28.128.0/24 is prefered, since this is to be used
for Lustre traffic:

 -- truncated --
 172.28.128.38 mds-1
 -- truncated --

Without the patch, `lnetctl ping mds-1` returns:

 ---
 manage:
 -     ping:
       errno: -22
       descr: ! "Invalid input data or parameter"
 ...

`lnetctl` is failing to correctly resolve the hostname.
With the patch:

 ping:
 - primary nid: 172.28.128.38@tcp
   Multi-Rail: false
   peer_ni:
   - nid: 172.28.128.38@tcp

Also, add a test to sanity-lnet to ensure this doesn't break
again in the future.

Test-Parameters: trivial testlist=sanity-lnet
Signed-off-by: Timothy Day <timday@amazon.com>
Change-Id: Ia27ea601c5e60d7bedad6a2013c48bb3a3fc7546
